title: Source-independence scoring: prevent multi-sourcing from being satisfied by correlated, echoed, or LLM-near-duplicate sources
context: PLAN.md v0.2 adds source-independence scoring as a first-class component of the verification layer. Multi-sourcing is defeated by correlated sources: if every source on a high-stakes claim is downstream of a single wrong primary, or if sources are LLM-generated echo-chamber near-duplicates, then 'three independent sources' gives false confidence. This is a recognized failure mode (citation cartels, SEO-spam, LLM content farms). ATLTUAE must detect shared-primary / echoed / LLM-generated near-duplicates so that a 'multi-sourced' claim cannot be satisfied by N copies of one claim. Source-independence scoring is also the backbone of quality-weighted coverage (atltuae-quality-coverage-031): a node's coverage quality degrades if its sources are correlated.
objective: Implement source-independence scoring in the verification layer (packages/core/verify): for each claim or node, compute a source-independence score that detects correlated sources (shared citation graph ancestry), echoed content (near-duplicate text after normalization), and LLM-generated near-duplicates (embedding-distance clustering); a high-stakes claim that relies on sources that fail the independence threshold is flagged and cannot count as 'multi-sourced' for the multi-source requirement; the independence score is stored in the provenance record and feeds the quality-weighted coverage computation.
acceptanceCriteria: Source-independence scoring detects at minimum: (a) citation-graph correlation (sources that share a common primary via traversal); (b) text-level near-duplicates (high overlap after normalization, targeting SEO-spam / mirrored content); (c) embedding-distance clustering (LLM-generated content farms where semantically identical claims appear across nominally different sources)

A high-stakes claim whose supporting sources all fall below the independence threshold is blocked from being counted as satisfying the multi-source requirement; the independence failure is recorded in the provenance record and surfaced in the verification report

The independence score for each source set is stored in the provenance record and emitted to the coverage ledger so that coverage metrics can be quality-weighted by source independence (feeds atltuae-quality-coverage-031)
A dedup/novelty test confirms that citation-cartel / near-duplicate source sets correctly score low and do not inflate the apparent multi-source count
Source-independence scoring is integrated into the verification layer (atltuae-verify-008) and covered by the red-team suite (atltuae-redteam-009)
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer (recorded)
